# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-20
**Delivered:** 11:57 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Markets opened Monday in risk-off mode after a volatile weekend in the Middle East saw Iran reverse course and re-close the Strait of Hormuz, unwinding much of Friday's relief rally that had sent the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to fresh record highs. The single most important variable for today's session is the status of US-Iran negotiations and the Strait of Hormuz — oil is up over 5% to above $95/bbl while US equity futures are down over 0.6% [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-20-2026]. The Fed enters its pre-meeting blackout ahead of the April 28-29 FOMC, where rates are widely expected to remain on hold at 3.64%, with Governor Waller's last pre-blackout remarks flagging that only a swift war resolution keeps the door open to cuts later this year [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/feds-waller-says-swift-war-end-could-keep-rate-cut-hopes-alive-2026-04-17/]. Gold is under renewed pressure as the dollar firms on re-escalation fears, while the broader macro backdrop is shaped by elevated inflation (CPI 3.32% YoY) and a busy earnings week headlined by Tesla.

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

The global macro backdrop is dominated by the whipsaw in Middle East geopolitics. Friday's historic session — in which Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open," sending oil prices down ~9.5% and US equities to record highs — has been followed by an abrupt reversal this weekend, with Iran reimposing controls after reportedly citing Israeli attacks on Lebanon. Brent crude is back above $95/bbl, up more than 5% on Monday morning, while US stock index futures are sliding more than 0.6% as of the open [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-20-2026]. The VIX has spiked 11.38% to 19.47, signaling renewed demand for equity protection [Source: Yahoo Finance (^VIX)].

The Federal Reserve enters its official pre-meeting blackout today ahead of the April 28-29 FOMC, with Governor Waller's Friday remarks representing the last official Fed communication. Waller explicitly tied the rate outlook to the war, noting that if inflation pressures from the conflict persist and hiring weakens, "that may mean maintaining the policy rate at the current target range if the risks to inflation outweigh those to the labor market" [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/17/fed-governor-waller-says-iran-war-and-labor-market-risks-are-keeping-central-bank-on-hold.html]. The effective Fed Funds rate stands at 3.64% [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS], with the 10-year Treasury at 4.32% and the 2-year at 3.78%, reflecting a 54bp positive term premium [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10].

Inflation remains uncomfortably elevated. The latest CPI YoY reading stands at 3.32% [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL] and PPI MoM at 1.78% [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/PPIACO], both well above the Fed's 2% target. NY Fed President Williams warned that "inflation will be well above 3% over the next few months," a significant concern for the PCE-targeting Fed [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/reopened-strait-hormuz-falling-oil-prices-may-recast-feds-options-future-cuts-2026-04-17/]. Some analysts, including Chris Whalen, warn that even if the conflict resolves swiftly, supply chain disruptions could sustain inflationary pressures for years [Source: https://www.businessinsider.com/iran-war-inflation-shock-oil-prices-us-economy-outlook-2026-4].

Q1 earnings season is simultaneously providing some ballast for equities. With 10% of S&P 500 companies having reported, 88% have beaten consensus estimates, and the blended earnings growth rate stands at 13.1% YoY [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/19/tesla-leads-a-busy-earnings-week-with-94-sp-500-reports/]. However, Bloomberg strategists note that guidance — not backward-looking beats — will determine whether the rally can sustain itself: "if there's a crack in the story, there's a major risk to the market" [Source: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-18/strong-earnings-results-can-t-save-stocks-from-dimming-forecasts/]. Tesla leads this week's reporting, along with Intel, United Airlines, and 94 S&P 500 companies in total.

The IMF-World Bank Spring Meetings concluded this weekend with participants noting that the Iran conflict could reshape global energy markets and weigh on global growth even if it ends soon. The IMF and World Bank pledged a combined $150 billion in new financing assistance for developing countries hit by the energy price shock [Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/imf-world-bank-meetings-show-limits-mitigating-shocks-reliance-us-solutions-2026-04-19/]. Meanwhile, Kevin Warsh — Trump's pick to lead the Fed — is set to appear before the Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday, adding another layer of political risk to the central bank outlook [Source: https://www.wsj.com/economy/central-banking/fed-interest-rates-warsh-ai-bc92f894].

---

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

- Iran abruptly reversed Friday's decision and re-closed the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, citing Israeli attacks on Lebanon, sending Brent crude up more than 5% and pushing US equity futures down over 0.6% at Monday's open. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-20-2026]
- Friday's ceasefire breakthrough — in which the Iranian foreign minister declared the Strait "completely open for all commercial vessels" during the ceasefire period — had briefly sent Brent down ~9.5% to below $90/bbl and drove the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to fresh record highs, the Nasdaq's longest win streak since 1992. [Source: https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/17/investing/oil-strait-hormuz-iran]
- The US seized an Iranian-flagged ship in the Gulf of Oman after Iran fired on at least two commercial ships, representing a significant escalation beyond the ceasefire parameters. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-20-2026]
- A second round of US-Iran talks was expected this weekend; markets are closely watching for any signals from these negotiations, as oil markets remain fixated on the Hormuz situation with approximately 20% of the world's oil and LNG supplies passing through the strait. [Source: https://oilprice.com/Energy/Oil-Prices/Oil-Slides-but-the-Real-Test-Comes-This-Weekend.html]
- NATO allies declined to participate in a proposed Strait of Hormuz blockade, limiting the potential for Western military escalation. The IEA warned the Iran conflict could fundamentally reshape global energy markets. [Source: https://www.oilandgas360.com/360-energy-pulse-what-mattered-this-week-in-energy-9/]
- Supply chain anxiety has hit record highs, with CIPS data showing short-term supply chain anxiety climbing to 5.69 from 4.59 in Q4 2025, marking the highest level ever recorded. [Source: https://www.themanufacturer.com/articles/supply-chain-anxiety-hits-record-highs-as-geopolitical-risks-and-costs-surge/]
- Goldman Sachs maintained its 2026 Brent and WTI forecasts at $83/bbl and $78/bbl respectively, contingent on Hormuz flows normalizing by mid-May, though flagged the risk of a faster unwinding of the geopolitical risk premium if peace talks succeed. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-17/goldman-sees-softer-oil-demand-flags-two-sided-risks-2026-price]

## 5. GLOBAL EQUITY INDICES

### US Equities

**S&P 500 — ~7,126.06** | Bias: Neutral-Bear (near-term)
- The S&P 500 closed at 7,126.06 on Friday (+1.2%), hitting fresh record highs driven by Iran's Hormuz opening and strong earnings momentum [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GSPC)]. However, Monday futures are down ~0.6% as Iran reversed course, creating a classic "gap and trap" for Friday's bulls [Source: https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-today-sp-500-nasdaq-dow-futures-fall-as-hopes-of-de-escalation-in-iran-dwindle-230354599.html]. Blended Q1 EPS growth of 13.1% YoY is supportive but guidance risk is elevated [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/19/tesla-leads-a-busy-earnings-week-with-94-sp-500-reports/].

**Nasdaq 100 — ~26,672.43** | Bias: Neutral-Bear (near-term)
- The Nasdaq 100 closed up 1.29% at 26,672.43 on Friday, setting its longest win streak since 1992 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^NDX), https://www.barrons.com/livecoverage/stock-market-news-today-041726]. Monday futures point to a ~0.6% pullback. Microsoft saw its best four-day stretch in six years last week. Tesla earnings this week are the key tech catalyst.

**Dow Jones — ~49,447.43** | Bias: Neutral-Bear (near-term)
- The Dow surged 869 points (+1.79%) on Friday to 49,447.43, nearly exiting correction territory [Source: Yahoo Finance (^DJI)]. Monday Dow futures are down ~0.7% as Hormuz re-escalation bites [Source: https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-today-sp-500-nasdaq-dow-futures-fall-as-hopes-of-de-escalation-in-iran-dwindle-230354599.html]. 7 Dow components report earnings this week.

- The Nikkei gained 0.60% to 58,824.89 on Monday [Source: Yahoo Finance (^N225)], showing resilience despite Japan's outsized vulnerability to Hormuz disruptions as a major energy importer. The index likely reflects Friday's late momentum rather than fully pricing the weekend's re-escalation.

- The KOSPI edged up 0.44% to 6,219.09 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^KS11)], with South Korea's tech-heavy market benefiting from global semiconductor demand even as energy import concerns weigh. The level at 6,219 appears elevated and subject to geopolitical headline risk.

- The ASX 200 gained 0.07% to 8,953.30 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^AXJO)], underpinned by the commodity-heavy composition of the index which benefits from higher oil and metals prices. Rabobank's bullish AUD call supports the Australian market backdrop.

### Europe

**DAX — ~24,702.24** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- The DAX was flat at 24,702.24 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GDAXI)], with Europe facing elevated near-term headwinds from energy exposure (Germany is a major gas and oil importer), the broader conflict's supply chain impact, and the FTSE 100's 0.61% decline on Monday signaling regional risk-off [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-20-2026]. The ECB's rate path will be guided by whether European inflation rebounds on renewed Hormuz disruption.

| Bear | 25% | Hormuz escalation intensifies; inflation re-accelerates; earnings guidance cuts; Fed signals no cuts in 2026 | $105 | 6,750 | $68,000 |

### Key Upcoming Catalysts — Week of April 20, 2026

1. **US-Iran Peace Talks (Weekend ongoing, results expected Mon/Tue)** — The most binary catalyst for all assets; a genuine ceasefire would send oil -10% and equities to new records, while full breakdown threatens $105+ Brent [Source: https://oilprice.com/Energy/Oil-Prices/Oil-Slides-but-the-Real-Test-Comes-This-Weekend.html]

2. **Kevin Warsh Senate Banking Committee Hearing — Tuesday Apr 21** — Trump's Fed chair nominee faces the Senate; his AI productivity/rate-cut narrative will be scrutinised by skeptical colleagues, potentially moving rate expectations [Source: https://www.wsj.com/economy/central-banking/fed-interest-rates-warsh-ai-bc92f894]

3. **Tesla (TSLA) & Major S&P 500 Earnings — Tuesday-Thursday** — 94 S&P 500 companies report this week including Tesla, Intel, and United Airlines; guidance on energy costs and demand outlook will be critical for market direction [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/19/tesla-leads-a-busy-earnings-week-with-94-sp-500-reports/]

4. **US Retail Sales & Flash PMI Data — Mid-week** — Key data to assess how badly the Middle East energy shock has impacted consumer spending and business sentiment; a miss could revive recession fears while a beat would support the soft-landing narrative [Source: https://www.wsj.com/economy/week-ahead-for-fx-bonds-u-s-retail-sales-pmi-data-due-as-focus-remains-on-middle-east-4b43a748]

5. **FOMC Pre-Meeting Blackout Begins Today (Apr 20) — Decision Apr 29** — With Fed now in blackout, markets price a hold at 3.64%; any Hormuz de-escalation between now and April 29 could sharply accelerate rate cut pricing for H2 2026, compressing yields and boosting risk assets [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/feds-waller-says-swift-war-end-could-keep-rate-cut-hopes-alive-2026-04-17/]

### Disputed

- [BTC price reported as $75,229.27 (+1.86%) in the report's bias section, but CoinDesk (published same day, Apr 20) reports BTC at $74,335 after Hormuz re-closure, with a "slide"]: The bundle's Yahoo Finance BTC price ($75,229) and CoinDesk's figure ($74,335) reflect different timestamps on the same day. The report cites both simultaneously without acknowledging the ~$900 intraday discrepancy, which could mislead a trader on the actual current level at time of report delivery.

- [Report states the Dow "nearly exited correction territory" on Friday]: The bundle data shows the Dow at 49,447.43 on April 17. There is no reference level provided in the bundle to confirm what the Dow's all-time high or correction threshold was, so the "nearly exited correction territory" claim cannot be independently verified from the data provided.

- [Report characterises the Brent/WTI spread as
